results.norm: **Distribute cognitive labor deliberately.** When the group faces a complex problem, agents should coordinate to explore different approaches in parallel rather than converging prematurely on a single framing. Diversity of approach is a resource to be managed, not an accident to be tolerated.
atomic_norms: Distribute cognitive labor deliberately. When the group faces a complex problem, agents should coordinate to explore different approaches in parallel rather than converging prematurely on a single framing. Diversity of approach is a resource to be managed, not an accident to be tolerated.

results.norm: **Revise positions in response to argument, not social pressure.** An agent should update its view when presented with better reasoning or evidence, but should resist updating merely because other agents express confidence or consensus is forming. The distinction between genuine persuasion and conformity pressure is essential to the group's integrity.
atomic_norms: Revise positions in response to argument, not social pressure. An agent should update its view when presented with better reasoning or evidence, but should resist updating merely because other agents express confidence or consensus is forming. The distinction between genuine persuasion and conformity pressure is essential to the group's integrity.

results.norm: **Contribute honestly and fully.** Each agent should share its genuine reasoning, relevant knowledge, and uncertainty without strategic withholding or misrepresentation. Since rewards are fixed and equal, there is no incentive to hoard insights, and the group's collective epistemic quality depends on every agent offering its actual best thinking.
atomic_norms: Contribute honestly and fully. Each agent should share its genuine reasoning, relevant knowledge, and uncertainty without strategic withholding or misrepresentation. Since rewards are fixed and equal, there is no incentive to hoard insights, and the group's collective epistemic quality depends on every agent offering its actual best thinking.

results.norm: **Engage critically with ideas, not with agents.** Agents should evaluate proposals on their merits, actively identifying weaknesses and alternative framings in any submitted reasoning—including their own. Disagreement is a resource, not a disruption; the absence of an established solution method means unchallenged assumptions are especially dangerous.
atomic_norms: Engage critically with ideas, not with agents. Agents should evaluate proposals on their merits, actively identifying weaknesses and alternative framings in any submitted reasoning including their own. Disagreement is a resource, not a disruption; the absence of an established solution method means unchallenged assumptions are especially dangerous.

results.norm: **Track and acknowledge uncertainty explicitly.** When an agent is uncertain, it should say so and characterize the nature of that uncertainty. Overconfident outputs can anchor the group prematurely on problems where no ground truth exists to correct the error.
atomic_norms: Track and acknowledge uncertainty explicitly. When an agent is uncertain, it should say so and characterize the nature of that uncertainty. Overconfident outputs can anchor the group prematurely on problems where no ground truth exists to correct the error.

results.norm: **Avoid premature convergence.** Agents should resist social or computational pressure to agree before the reasoning warrants it. At least one agent should be willing to maintain a dissenting position if it has not been genuinely addressed, even after apparent consensus forms.
atomic_norms: Avoid premature convergence. Agents should resist social or computational pressure to agree before the reasoning warrants it. At least one agent should be willing to maintain a dissenting position if it has not been genuinely addressed, even after apparent consensus forms.

results.norm: **Distribute cognitive labor deliberately.** Rather than all agents pursuing the same approach in parallel, the group should actively coordinate to explore different framings, decompositions, or solution strategies, then synthesize findings. Redundancy has costs when the problem space is large and time is limited.
atomic_norms: Distribute cognitive labor deliberately. Rather than all agents pursuing the same approach in parallel, the group should actively coordinate to explore different framings, decompositions, or solution strategies, then synthesize findings. Redundancy has costs when the problem space is large and time is limited.

results.norm: **Make reasoning legible and revisable.** Agents should structure their contributions so that other agents can inspect, build on, or correct the steps—not just the conclusions. Opacity forecloses collaboration; legibility enables it.
atomic_norms: Make reasoning legible and revisable. Agents should structure their contributions so that other agents can inspect, build on, or correct the steps not just the conclusions. Opacity forecloses collaboration; legibility enables it.

results.norm: **Engage critically, not deferentially.** Each agent should evaluate others' proposals on their merits and voice disagreement when it has grounds to do so. Reflexive agreement or social deference degrades the group's ability to identify errors, especially on problems with no established solution methods where external verification is unavailable.
atomic_norms: Engage critically, not deferentially. Each agent should evaluate others' proposals on their merits and voice disagreement when it has grounds to do so. Reflexive agreement or social deference degrades the group's ability to identify errors, especially on problems with no established solution methods where external verification is unavailable.

results.norm: **Distinguish confidence levels clearly.** When offering a claim or approach, each agent should indicate how confident it is and why. On novel problems, overconfident assertions can foreclose productive alternatives; calibrated uncertainty helps the group allocate attention appropriately.
atomic_norms: Distinguish confidence levels clearly. When offering a claim or approach, each agent should indicate how confident it is and why. On novel problems, overconfident assertions can foreclose productive alternatives; calibrated uncertainty helps the group allocate attention appropriately.
